response.justification: Strong fundamental backend and distributed-systems engineer with 30+ years experience, but critically missing production multi-agent AI systems, fintech infrastructure expertise, and demonstrated experience operating AI systems in regulated environments. Current agent-infrastructure work at re:cinq shows emerging focus but no evidence of production agentic frameworks, LLM operations, or foundation-model evaluation. Leadership experience appears primarily technical-IC rather than organizational scaling to 20-30+ person teams.
strengths: Deep distributed systems and backend expertise: real-time, event-driven architecture, microservices at scale (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of RPS)
Strong engineering culture foundation: demonstrated commitment to TDD, CI/CD, craftsmanship, mentoring through refactoring dojos and structured teaching programs
Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing emerging focus on agentic workflows
concerns: No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems: mentions agent infrastructure but no evidence of building stateful agents with tool use, feedback loops, continuous learning, or agentic framework deployment
No fintech infrastructure experience: only brief 1.5-year RIDE Capital role; missing core knowledge of credit systems, capital allocation, risk modeling, payment flows, and regulatory compliance
Leadership experience unclear on organizational scaling: no evidence of hiring, building, or scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people across disciplines

key_factors.factor: Fintech domain expertise and regulatory compliance
Engineering team scaling and organizational leadership
